name: HoloViews (Declarative Data Visualization)

performance.notes: Rendering depends on backend (matplotlib, bokeh, plotly); large datasets need Datashader

agent_readiness.notes: Declarative data visualization that separates data structure from presentation. Define Element types (Curve, Points, Image, etc.) and compose with * and +. Multiple backends: matplotlib, bokeh, plotly. Works with Jupyter. hvPlot provides pandas.plot-compatible API on top. Part of HoloViz ecosystem with Panel (dashboards), Datashader (big data rasterization), Param, GeoViews. Best for Jupyter-based exploratory analysis with backend flexibility.

gotchas: Can be slow without Datashader for large datasets
Backend switching can cause layout issues
Less intuitive than seaborn for quick plots
best_when: Jupyter-based exploratory analysis with multiple visualization backends
Building dashboards with Panel on top of HoloViews
avoid_when: Teams unfamiliar with declarative visualization paradigm
summary: HoloViews is a declarative data visualization library for Jupyter that separates data from presentation. Multiple backends (matplotlib, bokeh, plotly). Compose Elements with + and * operators. Part of HoloViz ecosystem with Panel (dashboards), Datashader (big data), and hvPlot (pandas API). Best for exploratory analysis needing backend flexibility or large dataset visualization via Datashader.
